body_txt: What do Michael Jackson , the ocean, and an atomic explosion have in common? Well, if you describe Michael Jackson as "somewhat famous", the ocean as "somewhat wet", and an atomic explosion as being "somewhat noisy", then you could say all three of those descriptions are "somewhat of an understatement." As such, an understatement, while technically accurate in a strictly literal sense, fails to convey the magnitude of the situation being described at best, and at worst reduces the entire affair to sheer hyperbole. There are many ways to use this. One common way is following the Rule of Three by having two strongly made points followed by something more casual . It may help to have a Deadpan Snarker who habitually presents things in the least exciting manner, especially if they've Seen It All . The Ditz may make understatements unintentionally. In any case, understatement only works where a tension level has been built to go "under". Note that like sarcasm , some people are completely blind to the effect . Often used by Captain Obvious . British and Australian humor occasionally demonstrates this. See also Worrisome "Mostly-ish" . Contrast Felony Misdemeanor . Please don't sinkhole this article in your own attempt at an understatement on the wiki. The attempt either works or it doesn't.
